# LDAP Connector - Full Documentation

# LDAP Connector 3.7

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) is a public standard that
facilitates maintenance and access to distributed directories (such as network user privilege
information) over an Internet Protocol (IP) network.

LDAP Connector supports LDAP over SSL (Deprecated), LDAP over TLS, and a Secure SSL connection type
for FIPS 140-3 compliant deployments.

Many LDAP servers are included in free-use open source projects and packages. The particulars of
installing, running, and configuring LDAP servers fall outside the scope of this document.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, see the LDAP Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The LDAP Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access an OpenLDAP instance that supports LDAP v3
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio version 7.0 or later

## Common Use Cases For the Connector

Common use cases for LDAP Connector include:

- Adding user accounts to Active Directory

  You can add business users to Active Directory groups defined on the base distinguished name (DN).

- Retrieving user attributes

  You can retrieve the basic attributes of a business user such as a userâs e-mail or phone number.

## Connection Types

LDAP Connector supports four types of connections:

- Basic

  Communications are not encrypted.

  > [!NOTE] If running in an instance configured with `mule.security.model=fips140-2`, attempting to
  > use a Basic connection returns an `LDAP:SECURITY` error.

- SSL (Deprecated)

  Communications are encrypted using Secure Sockets Layer (SSL). Accepts a custom truststore path
  for certificate validation. The SSL connection type is deprecated. Use the Secure SSL connection
  type instead.

- Secure SSL

  Communications are encrypted using SSL and accept a Mule TLS context factory for trust store
  configuration. This connection type is required for FIPS 140-3 compliant deployments, and is also
  supported in non-FIPS environments with any trust store type provided via **TLS Context**.

- TLS

  Communications are encrypted using Transport Layer Security (TLS).

You can use Basic (username and password) authentication for each of these connection types.

For more information about configuring connection types and authentication, see
[Using Studio to Configure LDAP Connector](ldap-connector-use-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [LDAP Connector Reference](ldap-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# LDAP Connector 3.7 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show how to use Anypoint Connector for Lightweight Directory Access Protocol
Connector (LDAP Connector) to:

- Add a group and user to an organizational unit and then delete that user and group
- Set up a common truststore

## Add and Delete Groups and Users from an Organizational Unit

This example shows how to use the LDAP **Add entry** operation to add a group and user to an
organizational unit and the **Delete entry** operation to delete a user and group from that
organizational unit.

The following screenshot shows the Studio app flow for this example:

![Flow for adding and deleting users](_images/ldap-usecase-flow.png)

### Add a Group and User to an Organizational Unit

To add a group and user to an organizational unit:

1.  Create a new Mule Project in Anypoint Studio.
2.  Add these properties to the `mule-artifact.properties` file:

    ```text
    config.principal.dn=<DN>
    config.password=<Password>
    config.url=<URL>
    ```

3.  Place the `mule-artifact.properties` file into the projectâs `src/main/resources` directory.
4.  From the **Mule Palette** view, click **HTTP** and drag the **Listener** source to the canvas.
5.  In the Listener properties window, change the display name of the **Listener** source to `HTTP`.
6.  Click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a global element.
7.  Accept the defaults.
8.  In the Listener properties window, set the **Path** field value to `/`.
9.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag the **Transform Message** component
    next to **HTTP**.
10. In the **Output** section, replace the existing text with the following DataWeave script:

    ```java
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    {
    	dn : "ou=DevOpsGroup," ++ attributes.queryParams.dn,
    	ou : "DevOpsGroup",
    	objectclass : ["top", "organizationalUnit"]
    }
    ```

11. From the **Mule Palette** view, select **LDAP** and drag the **Add entry** operation next to the
    **Transform Message** component to start adding an LDAP group.
12. Click **+** next to the connector configuration field to add a new global element.
13. Configure the global element as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration</p></td><td><p><code>LDAP_Configuration</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><p>User distinguished name (DN)</p></td><td><p><code>${config.principal.dn}</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>User password</p></td><td><p><code>${config.password}</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><p>URL that connects to the LDAP server</p></td><td><p><code>${config.url}</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

14. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect to the LDAP server instance.
15. Process the connection results as follows:
    - If the connection succeeds, click **OK** to save the configuration.
    - If the connection fails, review and correct any incorrect field values and test again.

16. Return to the LDAP Connector properties window and configure the parameters required for the
    **Add entry** operation:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Add Group Entry to LDAP Directory</code></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>LDAP Connector global element that you created previously</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code> (the default value)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

17. From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag the **Transform Message** component
    next to **Add Group Entry to the LDAP directory**.
18. In the **Output** section, replace the existing text with the following DataWeave script:

    ```java
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    {
    	dn : "cn=Test User,ou=DevOpsGroup," ++ attributes.queryParams.dn,
    	uid : "testUser",
    	cn : "Test User",
    	sn : "User",
    	userPassword : "test1234",
    	objectclass : ["top", "person", "organizationalPerson", "inetOrgPerson"]
    }
    ```

19. From the **Mule Palette** view, select **LDAP** and drag the **Add entry** operation next to
    **Transform Message** to start adding an LDAP user.
20. In the LDAP Connector properties editor, configure the parameters as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Add User Entry to LDAP Directory</code></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>LDAP Connector global element that you created previously</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code> (the default value)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Delete a Group and User from an Organizational Unit

After you successfully add a group and user to an organizational unit, delete them using LDAP
Connector:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **LDAP** and drag the **Delete entry** operation next to
    the **Add User Entry to LDAP Directory** on the canvas.
2.  In the LDAP Connector properties editor, configure the parameters as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Delete User Entry from LDAP Directory</code></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>LDAP Connector global element that you created previously</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><p><code>#['cn=Test User,ou=DevOpsGroup,' ++ attributes.queryParams.dn]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

3.  Drag another **Delete entry** operation next to the first one to start deleting the LDAP group
    entry.

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Delete Group Entry from LDAP Directory</code></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>LDAP Connector global element that you created previously</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><p><code>#['ou=DevOpsGroup,' ++ attributes.queryParams.dn]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Drag the **Transform Message** component next to **Delete Group Entry from LDAP Directory**.
5.  Set the payload to "Flow Successfully Completed".

### Test the App

To test the app:

1.  Save the project.
2.  Run the project as a Mule app.
3.  Send a PUT command to this URL:

    `http://localhost:8081/?dn=mulesoft,dc=orgrequest`

### XML for the Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ldap="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ldap" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ldap
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ldap/current/mule-ldap.xsd">
<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="74b34b41-4e8b-4b96-adec-da917b183083" >
<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
</http:listener-config>
<ldap:config name="LDAP_Configuration" doc:name="LDAP Configuration" doc:id="2e1caebb-d041-47ec-9513-69c140c78700" >
		<ldap:basic-connection authDn="${config.principal.dn}" authPassword="${config.password}" url="${config.url}" />
	</ldap:config>
	<flow name="ldap-examplesFlow" doc:id="dc01fbb8-1e2c-4255-be17-46309cdbef07" >
		<http:listener doc:name="HTTP" doc:id="180e5809-d508-498c-a74f-97d93afc98f8" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="183ca95a-dcc2-448f-8bba-26f7295fb1e4" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
	dn : "ou=DevOpsGroup," ++ attributes.queryParams.dn,
	ou : "DevOpsGroup",
	objectclass : ["top", "organizationalUnit"]
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<ldap:add doc:name="Add Group Entry to LDAP Directory" doc:id="95d60199-e087-4fe1-afa7-1c035fdd3aec" config-ref="LDAP_Configuration"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="0e96e606-48be-4ba0-9266-242bd9911184">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[{
	dn : "cn=Test User,ou=DevOpsGroup," ++ attributes.queryParams.dn,
	uid : "testUser",
	cn : "Test User",
	sn : "User",
	userPassword : "test1234",
	objectclass : ["top", "person", "organizationalPerson", "inetOrgPerson"]
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<ldap:add doc:name="Add User Entry to LDAP Directory" doc:id="28c98048-0ba6-4a36-92df-49d437303217" config-ref="LDAP_Configuration"/>
		<ldap:delete doc:name="Delete User Entry from LDAP Directory" doc:id="07e7141a-03d9-4565-bbca-8a4b03cbb6ee" config-ref="LDAP_Configuration" dn="#['cn=Test User,ou=DevOpsGroup,' ++ attributes.queryParams.dn]"/>
		<ldap:delete doc:name="Delete Group ENtry from LDAP Directory" doc:id="892dca59-86a3-40a2-b758-09560fb95383" config-ref="LDAP_Configuration" dn="#['ou=DevOpsGroup,' ++ attributes.queryParams.dn]"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="5dc391aa-87b1-4e84-97e5-71dc5f8daa22" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Set Up a Common Truststore

This example shows how to change the previous example so that it has a TLS configuration that uses a
custom truststore.

> [!NOTE] The custom truststore feature is not FIPS compliant.

To set up a custom truststore:

1.  Follow the steps in the previous example.
2.  Add these properties to the `mule-artifact.properties` file to hold your LDAP credentials:

    ```text
    config.principal.dn=<DN>
    config.password=<Password>
    config.url=<URL>
    ```

3.  Place the `mule-artifact.properties` file into the projectâs `src/main/resources` directory.
4.  Locate the following element in the XML code:

    ```xml
     <ldap:conf name=:LDAP_configuration" doc:name="LDAP Configuration"
     .
     .
     .
     </ldap:config
    ```

5.  Overlay the located element with the following code:

    ```xml
    <ldap:config name="LDAP_Configuration">
      <ldap:tls-connection
        authDn="${config.principal.dn}}"
        authPassword="${config.password}"
        url="${config.url}"
        trustStorePath="path_to_truststore_jks_file"
        trustStorePassword="changeit" />
    ```

### Test the App

To test the app:

1.  Save the project.
2.  Run the project as a Mule app.
3.  Send a PUT command to this URL:

    `http://localhost:8081/?dn=mulesoft,dc=orgrequest`

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# LDAP Connector 3.7 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) enables you to interface with
LDAP servers.

## Default Configuration

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#basic-configuration-type">Basic Configuration Type</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ssl-configuration-type-deprecated">SSL Configuration Type (Deprecated)</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#secure-ssl-configuration-type">Secure SSL Configuration Type</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#tls-configuration-type">TLS Configuration Type</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p>ExpirationPolicy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Attributes As Objects</p></td><td><p>boolean</p></td><td><p>If set to true, the queries display the entity attributes as objects, containing the definition type, a flag to indicate whether or not the attribute is multi-value, and the value of the attribute. If set to false, the queries only display the attribute values.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Basic Configuration Type

For the Basic Configuration Type connection, the communications are not encrypted. If LDAP Connector
runs in an instance configured with `mule.security.model=fips140-2`, attempting to use a Basic
Configuration Type connection returns the `LDAP:SECURITY` error.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN (distinguished name) of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the authentication mechanism to use. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>simple</code> - Authenticates using the user DN and password</p></li><li><p><code>none</code> - Does not authenticate</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><code>simple</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection URL to the LDAP server.</p><p>You can configure <strong>URL</strong> to specify multiple LDAP hosts, for example, <code>ldap://ldap1.example.com ldap2.example.com/ou=intern-users,dc=acc,o=myorg</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Enumeration, one of:</p><p>** JNDI</p></td><td><p>The implementation of the connection to use.</p></td><td><p>JNDI</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to true, the LDAP connector uses the LDAP schema (only works for LDAP v3) to define the structure of the LDAP entry or map. This must be 'true' to use DataSense because it affects the implementing class of org.mule.module.ldap.api.LDAPEntry attributes.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Referral</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IGNORE</p></li><li><p>THROW</p></li><li><p>FOLLOW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Constant that holds the name of the environment property for specifying how referrals encountered by the service provider are to be processed (follow, ignore, throw).</p></td><td><p>IGNORE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Extended Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is a Map instance holding extended configuration attributes to use in the Context environment. When working with TLS connections make sure that the native LDAP pooling functionality is turned off. For example, if using JNDI, do not use attributes such as <code>com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool=true</code>, which causes problems when using TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Pool Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The string representation of an integer that represents the number of connections per connection identity to create when initially creating a connection for the identity. To disable pooling, just set this value to 0 (zero).</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Pool Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The string representation of an integer that represents the maximum number of connections per connection identity that can be maintained concurrently.</p></td><td><p>5</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pool Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The string representation of an integer that represents the number of milliseconds that an idle connection may remain in the pool without being closed and removed from the pool.</p></td><td><p>60000</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] To see more logs related to connection pools, use
> `com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.debug=all`.

## SSL Configuration Type (Deprecated)

> [!IMPORTANT] The **SSL Configuration** type is deprecated. Use the
> [Secure SSL Configuration Type](#secure-ssl-configuration-type) instead, which accepts a Mule TLS
> context factory and supports FIPS 140-3 compliant deployments.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN (distinguished name) of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the authentication mechanism to use. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>simple</code> - Authenticates using the user DN and password</p></li><li><p><code>none</code> - Does not authenticate</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><code>simple</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection URL to the LDAP server.</p><p>You can configure <strong>URL</strong> to specify multiple LDAP hosts, for example, <code>ldap://ldap1.example.com ldap2.example.com/ou=intern-users,dc=acc,o=myorg</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JNDI</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The implementation of the connection to use.</p></td><td><p>JNDI</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the LDAP connector will use the LDAP schema (works only for LDAP v3) to define the structure of the LDAP entry or map. This parameter must be set to <code>true</code> to use DataSense, as it affects the implementing class of <code>org.mule.module.ldap.api.LDAPEntry</code> attributes.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Referral</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IGNORE</p></li><li><p>THROW</p></li><li><p>FOLLOW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A constant that consists of the name of the environment property to specify how referrals encountered by the service provider must be processed (FOLLOW, IGNORE, THROW).</p></td><td><p>IGNORE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Extended Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>A map instance that consists of extended configuration attributes used in the context environment. When working with TLS connections, ensure that the native LDAP pooling functionality is turned off. For example, if using JNDI, do not use attributes such as <code>com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool=true</code>, as it will cause problems when using TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TrustStore Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom path to the truststore that contains the certificates needed for a secure authentication. The supported formats are JKS and PKCS12.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TrustStore Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom password for the custom truststore file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Secure SSL Configuration Type

SSL connection provider that accepts a Mule TLS context factory for trust store configuration. This
connection type is required for FIPS 140-3 compliant deployments, and is also usable in non-FIPS
environments with any trust store type supplied via **TLS Context** field.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>TLS Context</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TLS">TLS Type</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The TLS context used to configure secure communication.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN (distinguished name) of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the authentication mechanism to use. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>simple</code> - Authenticates using the user DN and password</p></li><li><p><code>none</code> - Does not authenticate</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p><code>simple</code></p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection URL to the LDAP server.</p><p>You can configure <strong>URL</strong> to specify multiple LDAP hosts, for example, <code>ldap://ldap1.example.com ldap2.example.com/ou=intern-users,dc=acc,o=myorg</code>.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>x</p></td><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JNDI</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The implementation of the connection to use.</p></td><td><p>JNDI</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Schema Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the LDAP connector will use the LDAP schema (works only for LDAP v3) to define the structure of the LDAP entry or map. This parameter must be set to <code>true</code> to use DataSense, as it affects the implementing class of <code>org.mule.module.ldap.api.LDAPEntry</code> attributes.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Referral</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IGNORE</p></li><li><p>THROW</p></li><li><p>FOLLOW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A constant that consists of the name of the environment property to specify how referrals encountered by the service provider must be processed (FOLLOW, IGNORE, THROW).</p></td><td><p>IGNORE</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Extended Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>A map instance that consists of extended configuration attributes used in the context environment. When working with TLS connections, ensure that the native LDAP pooling functionality is turned off. For example, if using JNDI, do not use attributes such as <code>com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool=true</code>, as it will cause problems when using TLS.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## TLS Configuration Type

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN (distinguished name) of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the authentication mechanism to use. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>simple</code> - Authenticates using the user DN and password</p></li><li><p><code>none</code> - Does not authenticate</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><code>simple</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection URL to the LDAP server.</p><p>You can configure <strong>URL</strong> to specify multiple LDAP hosts, for example, <code>ldap://ldap1.example.com ldap2.example.com/ou=intern-users,dc=acc,o=myorg</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JNDI</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The implementation of the connection to use.</p></td><td><p>JNDI</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to true, the LDAP connector uses the LDAP schema (only works for LDAP v3) to define the structure of the LDAP entry (or map). This needs to be 'true' to use DataSense as it affects the implementing class of <code>org.mule.module.ldap.api.LDAPEntry</code> attributes.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Referral</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IGNORE</p></li><li><p>THROW</p></li><li><p>FOLLOW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Constant that holds the name of the environment property for specifying how referrals encountered by the service provider are to be processed (follow, ignore, throw).</p></td><td><p>IGNORE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Extended Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is a Map instance holding extended configuration attributes to use in the Context environment. When working with TLS connections, you need to ensure that the native LDAP pooling functionality is turned off. For example if using JNDI, do not use attributes such as <code>com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool=true</code>, which causes problems when using TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TrustStore Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom path to the truststore that contains the certificates needed for a secure authentication. The supported formats are JKS and PKCS12.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TrustStore Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom password for the custom truststore file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Add Entry](#add)
- [Add Multi Value Attribute](#addMultiValueAttribute)
- [Add Single Value Attribute](#addSingleValueAttribute)
- [Bind](#bind)
- [Delete Entry](#delete)
- [Delete Multi Value Attribute](#deleteMultiValueAttribute)
- [Delete Single Value Attribute](#deleteSingleValueAttribute)
- [Exists](#exists)
- [LDAPEntry To LDIF](#ldapEntryToLdif)
- [Lookup](#lookup)
- [Modify Entry](#modify)
- [Modify Multi Value Attribute](#modifyMultiValueAttribute)
- [Modify Single Value Attribute](#modifySingleValueAttribute)
- [Paged Result Search](#pagedResultSearch)
- [Rename entry](#rename)
- [Search](#ldapsearch)
- [Search One](#searchOne)
- [Unbind](#unbind)

## Associated Sources

- [On Modified Objects](#modifiedObjects)
- [On New Objects](#newObjects)

## Operations

## Add Entry

`<ldap:add>`

Creates a new LDAPEntry in the LDAP server. The entry should contain the distinguished name (DN),
the objectClass attributes that define its structure and at least a value for all the required
attributes. Required attributes depend on the object classes assigned to the entry. Refer to RFC
4519 for standard object classes and attributes.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The LDAPEntry that should be added.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to add. If the entry doesnât have the objectClass attribute set, then this one is used to retrieve the whole objectClass hierarchy. If performance is a requirement, donât rely on this functionality, as several calls to the LDAP server are done to traverse the object class hierarchy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Add Multi Value Attribute

`<ldap:add-multi-value-attribute>`

Adds all the values for an attribute in an existing LDAP entry. If the entry already contains a
value (or values) for an attributeName, then these values are added. The attribute should allow
multiple values, or an exception is raised.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to add values to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Values</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The values for the attribute.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute value to add is already present, then donât throw the INVALID_ATTRIBUTE error.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Add Single Value Attribute

`<ldap:add-single-value-attribute>`

Adds a value for an attribute in an existing LDAP entry. If the entry already contains a value for
the given attributeName, then this value is added (only if the attribute is multi value and the
entry didnât have the value already). If you want to add a value with a type different than String,
then you can use the add-multi-value-attribute operation and define a single element list with the
value.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to add a value to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value for the attribute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute value to add is already present, then donât throw InvalidAttributeException.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Bind

`<ldap:bind>`

Performs an LDAP bind (login) operation. After login there will be an LDAP connection pool ready to
use for other operations using the authenticated user. If no values are provided to override authDn
and authPassword then using this operation will just re-bind (re-authenticate) the user/password
defined in the config element. If new values are provided for authDn and authPassword, then
authentication will be performed. Re-authenticating and returning the LDAP entry using config level
credentials (authDn & authPassword).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Principal DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Principal DN of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Password for Principal DN.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the authentication mechanism to use. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>simple</code> - Authenticates using the user DN and password</p></li><li><p><code>none</code> - Does not authenticate</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><code>simple</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Delete Entry

`<ldap:delete>`

Deletes the LDAP entry represented by the provided distinguished name (DN). The entry should not
have child entries, in which case a CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY error is thrown. This operation is idempotent.
The operations succeeds even if the terminal atomic name is not bound in the target context, but
throws NAME_NOT_FOUND error if any of the intermediate contexts do not exist.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Delete Multi Value Attribute

`<ldap:delete-multi-value-attribute>`

Deletes all the values matching attributeValues of the attribute defined by attributeName. Values
that are not present in the entry are ignored. If no values are specified, then the whole attribute
is deleted from the entry.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to delete its values.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Values</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The values that should be deleted.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute or value to delete is not present, then donât throw the INVALID_ATTRIBUTE error.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Delete Single Value Attribute

`<ldap:delete-single-value-attribute>`

Deletes the value matching attributeValue of the attribute defined by attributeName. If the entry
didnât have the value, then the entry stays the same. If no value is specified, then the whole
attribute is deleted from the entry. If you want to delete a value with a type different than
String, then you can use the delete-multi-value-attribute operation and define a single element list
with the value.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to delete its value.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value that should be deleted.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute or value to delete is not present, then donât throw the INVALID_ATTRIBUTE error.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Exists

`<ldap:exists>`

Checks whether an LDAP entry exists in the LDAP server or not.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## LDAPEntry To LDIF

`<ldap:ldap-entry-to-ldif>`

Transforms an LDAPEntry to a String in LDIF representation (RFC 2849).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The LDAPEntry to transform to LDIF.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Lookup

`<ldap:lookup>`

Retrieves an entry from the LDAP server based on its distinguished name (DN). Distinguished Names
are the unique identifiers of an LDAP entry, so this method performs a search based on this ID and
returns a single entry as the result, or throws an exception if the DN is invalid or doesnât exist.

When you know the DN of the object you want to retrieve, use this operation:

`#searchOne(LDAPConfiguration, LDAPConnectionWrapper, String, String, List, SearchScope, int, long, boolean, String)`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of the attributes to return in the result. If the attributes list is empty or null, then by default all LDAP entry attributes are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to return. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Modify Entry

`<ldap:modify>`

Updates an existing LDAPEntry in the LDAP server. The entry should contain an existing distinguished
name (DN), and at least a value for all the required attributes. Required attributes depend on the
object classes assigned to the entry. You can refer to RFC 4519 for standard object classes and
attributes.

When updating an LDAP entry, only the attributes in the entry passed as parameters are updated or
added. If you need to delete an attribute, you should use the delete attribute operation.

Example: Updating one attribute and adding another.

Original LDAP server entry:

```text
dn: cn=entry,ou=group,dc=company,dc=org
cn: entry
attr1: Value1
attr2: Value2
multi1: Value3
multi1: Value4
objectclass: top
objectclass: myentry
```

Entry map passed as a parameter:

```text
dn: cn=entry,ou=group,dc=company,dc=org
attr1: NewValue
attr3: NewAttributeValue `
```

Resulting LDAP server entry:

```text
dn: cn=entry,ou=group,dc=company,dc=org
cn: entry
attr1: NewValue
attr2: Value2
multi1: Value3
multi1: Value4
attr3: NewAttributeValue
objectclass: top
objectclass: myentry
```

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The LDAPEntry that should be updated.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to update. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Modify Multi Value Attribute

`<ldap:modify-multi-value-attribute>`

Updates (replaces) the value or values of the attribute defined by attributeName with the new values
defined by attributeValues. If the attribute is not present in the entry, then the value is added.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to update its values.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Values</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The new values for the attribute.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute value to modify is already present, then donât throw the INVALID_ATTRIBUTE error.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Modify Single Value Attribute

`<ldap:modify-single-value-attribute>`

Updates (replaces) the value or values of the attribute defined by attributeName with the new value
defined by attributeValue. If the attribute is not present in the entry, then the value is added. To
update a value with a type different than String, use the update-multi-value-attribute operation and
define a single element list with the value.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DN of the LDAP entry to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the attribute to update its value.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attribute Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The new value for the attribute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Invalid Attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the attribute value to modify is already present, then donât throw the INVALID_ATTRIBUTE error.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Paged Result Search

`<ldap:paged-result-search>`

Performs an LDAP search and streams the result to the rest of the flow.

This means that if the LDAP server supports paging, this operation chunks the LDAP search request in
pages, and then returns a list with all the results to the rest of the flow.

For queries returning large results, it is best to use pagination, however, not all LDAP servers
support this or are configured to support it.

To use pagination, provide a page size value thatâs less than or equal to the **Max Results** (count
limit). If you get a size limit exceeded exception, ensure that the authenticated user has
sufficient privileges, or that the LDAP server is not limited by its configuration, in which case,
you should reduce the value of the fetch size.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The base DN of the LDAP search.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A valid LDAP filter. LDAP connector supports LDAP search filters as defined in RFC 2254.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of the attributes to return in the result. If the attributes list is empty or null then, by default, all LDAP entry attributes are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OBJECT</p></li><li><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></li><li><p>SUB_TREE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the search. Valid attributes are:</p><p>* OBJECT: Searches only the entry at the base DN so that only results from that entry are returned. This must meet the search filter criteria. * ONE_LEVEL: Searches all entries one level under the base DN and does not include entries in the base DN or any entries one level under the base DN. * SUB_TREE: Searches all entries at all levels under and including the specified base DN.</p></td><td><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Search timeout in milliseconds. If the value is 0, this means to wait indefinitely.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of entries to return as a result of the search. 0 indicates to return all entries.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Return Object</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables or disables returning objects that are returned as part of the result. If disabled, only the name and class of the object is returned. If enabled, the object is returned.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the LDAP server supports paging results, set this attribute to the size of the page. If the pageSize is less than or equal to 0, paging is disabled.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order by attribute</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the LDAP attribute used to sort results.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ascending order?</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If orderBy is set, indicates whether to sort in ascending or descending order.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to return. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fetch Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of LDAP entries retrieved at once per page.</p></td><td><p>200</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>repeatable-in-memory-iterable</p></li><li><p>repeatable-file-store-iterable</p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Rename entry

`<ldap:rename>`

Renames an existing LDAP entry (moves an entry from a DN to another one).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Current DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>DN of the existing entry to rename.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>New DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Destination DN</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Search

`<ldap:search>`

Performs an LDAP search that returns a list of all resulting LDAP entries. For queries returning
large results, use pagination; however, not all LDAP servers support this or are configured to
support it. To use pagination, provide a page size value thatâs less than or equal to the max
results (count limit). If you get a size limit exceeded exception, ensure that the authenticated
user has sufficient privileges, or that the LDAP server is not limited by its configuration.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The base DN of the LDAP search.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A valid LDAP filter. The LDAP connector supports LDAP search filters as defined in RFC 2254.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of the attributes to return in the result. If the attributes list is empty or null, by default all LDAP entry attributes are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OBJECT</p></li><li><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></li><li><p>SUB_TREE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the search. Valid attributes are:</p><p>* OBJECT: This value is used to indicate searching only the entry at the base DN, resulting in only that entry being returned (keeping in mind that it also has to meet the search filter criteria) * ONE_LEVEL: This value is used to indicate searching all entries one level under the base DN - but not including the base DN and not including any entries under that one level under the base DN. * SUB_TREE: This value is used to indicate searching of all entries at all levels under and including the specified base DN.</p></td><td><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Search timeout in milliseconds. If the value is 0, this means to wait indefinitely.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of entries to return as a result of the search. 0 indicates to return all entries.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Return Object</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables or disables objects returned as part of the result. If disabled, only the name and class of the object is returned. If enabled, the object is also returned.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the LDAP server supports paging results, set this attribute to the size of the page. If the pageSize is less than or equal to 0, then paging is disabled.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to return. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Search One

`<ldap:search-one>`

Performs an LDAP search that is supposed to return a unique result. If the search returns more than
one result, a warning log message is generated and the first element of the result is returned. Use
this operation over `#lookup(LDAPConfiguration, LDAPConnectionWrapper, String, List, String)` when
you donât know the DN of the entry you need to retrieve but have a set of attributes that you know
should return a single entry (for example an email address).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The base DN of the LDAP search.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A valid LDAP filter. The LDAP connector supports LDAP search filters as defined in RFC 2254.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of the attributes to return in the result. If the attributes list is empty or null, then by default all LDAP entry attributes are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OBJECT</p></li><li><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></li><li><p>SUB_TREE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the search. Valid attributes are:</p><p>* OBJECT: Indicates to search only for the entry at the base DN, resulting in only that entry being returned (keep in mind that it also has to meet the search filter criteria). * ONE_LEVEL: Indicates to search for all entries one level under the base DN - but not including the base DN and not including any entries under that one level under the base DN. * SUB_TREE: Indicates to search for all entries at all levels under and including the specified base DN.</p></td><td><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Search timeout in milliseconds. If the value is 0, this means to wait indefinitely.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of entries to return as a result of the search. 0 indicates to return all entries.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Return Object</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables or disables returning objects returned as part of the result. If disabled, only the name and class of the object is returned. If enabled, the object also returns.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to return. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable in which the operationâs output is placed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Unbind

`<ldap:unbind>`

Closes the current connection, forcing the login operation (bind) the next time it is used.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- LDAP:COMMUNICATION
- LDAP:CONNECTIVITY
- LDAP:SECURITY
- LDAP:CONTEXT_NOT_EMPTY
- LDAP:INVALID_ATTRIBUTE
- LDAP:INVALID_ENTRY
- LDAP:NAME_ALREADY_BOUND
- LDAP:NAME_NOT_FOUND
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED
- LDAP:OPERATION_NOT_COMPLETED
- LDAP:PERMISSION
- LDAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- LDAP:UNKNOWN

## Sources

## On Modified Objects

`<ldap:modified-objects>`

Triggers when an existing LDAP entry is modified.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base DN</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The base DN of the LDAP search.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A valid LDAP filter. The LDAP connector supports LDAP search filters as defined in RFC 2254.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of the attributes to return in the result. If the attributes list is empty or null, then by default all LDAP entry attributes are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OBJECT</p></li><li><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></li><li><p>SUB_TREE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the search. Valid attributes are:</p><p>* OBJECT: Searches only the entry at the base DN so that only results from that entry are returned. This must meet the search filter criteria. * ONE_LEVEL: Searches all entries one level under the base DN and does not include entries in the base DN or any entries one level under the base DN. * SUB_TREE: Searches all entries at all levels under and including the specified base DN.</p></td><td><p>ONE_LEVEL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Structural Object Class</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of entry to return. This is used for DataSense in Anypoint Studio IDE and has no impact on Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify a date in <code>YYYYMMDDhhmmssâZ'</code> format, which is the way LDAP formats dates (for example, <code>20170317163040Z</code>).
